pages:
ConstructIQ Solutions Inc.
Portfolio Update | Q2 2025 | Construction Project Management SaaS
Period: Q2 2025
ConstructIQ provides cloud-based project management and compliance tracking software to general contractors and
subcontractors across North America. Pricing is per-seat with add-on modules for safety compliance, bid management, and
subcontractor portals.
Headline Metrics
Metric Q2 2025 Q1 2025
Annual Recurring Revenue $18.6M $17.1M
Quarterly Recognized Revenue $4.5M $4.2M
Gross Margin 71% 70%
Net Revenue Retention (LTM) 112% 110%
Logo Churn (LTM) 6.3% 6.8%
Total Paying Accounts 834 798
Avg. Modules per Account 2.4 2.1
Total Headcount 89 84
Cash & Equivalents $11.2M $12.1M
Quarterly Net Burn ($0.91M) ($0.97M)
Platform Usage Metrics - Q2 2025
Usage Metric Q2 2025
Monthly Active Projects (avg.) 12,400
Daily Active Users / Licensed Seats 58%
Documents Processed (QTD) 2.1M
Safety Inspection Completions (QTD) 94,000
Subcontractor Portal Connections 3,810
Average module attach increased to 2.4 from 2.1 in Q1 2025, driven by adoption of the safety compliance module following new
OSHA reporting mandates in three U.S. states. Daily active user rate of 58% remains below management's internal 65% target.
Pipeline & Business Update
During Q2 2025, ConstructIQ completed the migration of 47 legacy on-premise customers to the cloud platform. Three enterprise
accounts (each >$200k ARR) are in late-stage negotiation; one has issued a verbal commitment. Weighted pipeline stands at
$6.4M. Cash runway is approximately 12 months at current burn; management is evaluating a Series B process.
(1) ARR reflects signed, active contracts only; excludes month-to-month arrangements (<2% of accounts).

(2) Net Revenue Retention computed on a last-twelve-months basis.
(3) Daily Active User rate is a 30-day trailing average at period end.
(4) Cash runway does not account for potential proceeds from the Series B process under evaluation.

metrics.source_quote: Cash runway is approximately 12 months at current burn; management is evaluating a Series B process.
